在一個完整的句子前加上 that，可將其變成名詞子句。that 引導的名詞子句，可置於表達想法或感受的動詞後面，此類動詞有 think、know、believe、find 等。
that 引導的名詞子句，亦可置於表達情緒或感受的形容詞後面，此類形容詞有 surprised、 worried、sure、angry 等。
當兩個 that 引導的名詞子句用對等連接詞 and 連接時，第二個名詞子句的 that 不可省略。
examples: I think (that) Melissa is really outgoing and friendly.
Bonnie was surprised (that) I came to work late today.
I know (that) Hans studied hard and that he got a very good grade on the test.

grammar.formula: ...N(,) + where/when + S + V....
grammar.explanation:
此句型為關係副詞 where 和 when 所引導的形容詞子句，與關係代名詞引導的形容詞子句相同，有「限定」與「非限定」兩種用法。
1.  當先行詞為表示「地點或場所」的名詞時，關係副詞用 where。
2.   當先行詞為表示「時間」的名詞時，關係副詞用 when。
examples: The city where we spent our honeymoon is quite beautiful.
Here’s a picture of the spectacular Eiffel Tower, where Jeff asked Marina to marry him.
If you want me to give you a ride, tell me the exact time when you’ll arrive.
Henry died in 1970, when his third daughter was born.

The following is your diary entry about the trip last Sunday. Underline the six things that you wish you could have changed that day and use the above pattern to describe them. The first one has been done for you.

It was a pleasant Sunday. Out of the blue, Dad decided to take us to the beach, so we hurried to get everything ready. Strangely, it took me forever to find my swimming suit. Meanwhile, Jason and Judy, my little brother and sister, were fighting over the beach toys, so they both ended up in tears.

When we had gotten halfway to our destination, the car ran out of gas and stopped. We had no choice but to push it to the nearest gas station. By the time we got to the beach, all of us were dog-tired. Suddenly, I heard Judy crying like crazy. As it turned out, she’d forgotten to bring her favorite beach toy. It took us forever to get her to stop crying. When Jason complained about his rumbling* stomach, we decided to take a lunch break. It was then that Mom realized she had forgotten to pack a lunch. As there were no shops nearby, we had to walk for thirty minutes to find a restaurant.

When we headed back to the beach, it suddenly began to pour. We didn’t bring umbrellas and got soaking* wet as a result. Dad decided to return home, but Jason was unwilling to go and threw a fit*. When we finally reached our doorstep, we couldn’t find the key to our house. As we waited for the locksmith*, I heard Jason and Judy weeping bitterly and saw tears roll down my parents’ cheeks.
If Jason and Judy hadn’t been fighting over the beach toys, they wouldn’t have ended up in tears.

grammar.formula: S + must/may/might/could + VR/have + p.p.....
grammar.explanation: 此句型是用情態助動詞推測某事件的可能性，此類情態助動詞有 must、may、might、could，依所推測事件的時間點，分為兩類：

grammar.formula: 推測「非過去」事件的可能性：S + must/may/might/could + VR....
examples: His last name is Lafayette. He may be French.

grammar.formula: (2) 推測「過去」事件的可能性：S + must/may/might/could + have + p.p.....
grammar.explanation: 此句型中的情態助動詞，依照說話者的確定程度，由高而低依序是must、may、might/could。
examples: That scientist doesn’t have a degree in physics. He could have learned the subject by himself.
Larry’s sister is wearing a ring on her left ring finger. She must be married.
Ben’s boss doesn’t tell him everything. He may not know about the plan.
The couple were wandering the narrow alleys. They might/could have been lost.
